title: senecio teneriffae
intro: Senecio teneriffae, also known as Canary Islands ragwort, is a flowering plant species native to the Canary Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its alkaloid compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and acting as a diuretic. The herbal preparations of Senecio teneriffae include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
